revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->
{{infobox religious building | name = Heguri Shrine (Kuwana)|native_name = Heguri Shrine (平群神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image = File:Heguri-jinja, torii-1.jpg | address = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kuwana, Mie|en|Kuwana, Mie|qid=Q496472|lt=Kuwana, Mie|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, Shichi 3693 | 緯度度 = 35| 緯度分 = 3| 緯度秒 = 5.5 | 経度度 = 136| 経度分 = 36| 経度秒 = 50 | 祭神 = {{ill|Heguri Tsukunonokami|ja|平群木菟宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri Tsukunonokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平群木菟|1=Heguri no Tsuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17216122}} | 神体 = | 社格 = {{ill|Engishiki Shrine|ja|延喜式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|lt=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}（Former classification: {{ill|Gōsha (village shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}） | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = October 11-12 | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Mie | location = {{ill|Heguri|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.62247222222222|N|135.70397222222223|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Heguri Shrine''' (平群神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kuwana, Mie|en|Kuwana, Mie|qid=Q496472|lt=Kuwana, Mie|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is classified as an {{ill|Engishiki Shrine|ja|延喜式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|lt=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and was designated a {{ill|Gōsha (village shrine)|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in {{ill|Meiji 33|ja|明治三十三年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji 33|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, having been elevated from a {{ill|Murasha (village shrine)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha (village shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}.
== History ==
The exact date of Heguri Shrine's establishment is unknown. The shrine is located on the eastern slope of a hill known as Heguri-yama (or Chige-no-yama), which is believed to be a Kofun {{sfn|桑名市史}}.<ref>The site has yielded earthenware, Haji pottery, and Sue pottery, drawing archaeological interest.</ref> The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|ja|平群木菟宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Tsuku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平群木菟|1=Heguri no Tsuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17216122}}, the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Heguri clan|ja|平群氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri clan|1=Heguri Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483498}}. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he was born on the same day as {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}.
Heguri Shrine is also considered a site of {{ill|Mountain worship|ja|山岳崇拝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain worship|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as the mountain itself was believed to be a deity.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.jinja-net.jp/jinjacho-mie/jsearch3mie.php?jinjya=2868 |title=Heguri Shrine |access-date=2024-4-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|和書 |title=桑名市史 |publisher=}}</ref> It is said that the descendants of Tsukunonokami received the surname {{ill|Umazake no Omi|ja|味酒臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umazake no Omi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, migrated to {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}, and settled in this region, where they enshrined their ancestor.
Additional deities enshrined include: * {{ill|Takenouchi no Sukune|en|Takenouchi no Sukune|qid=Q1073668|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} (father of Tsukunonokami) * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} (merged with Kushida Shrine in 1910)
This site is also linked to the legendary journey of Yamato Takeru, who is said to have rested here.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081203-01.html |title=Heguri Shrine |access-date=2024-4-15}}</ref> His famous poem:
''"Those who live long and well, Should take a leaf of the great Kashi tree From the many-folded Heguri Mountain And wear it as an ornament."'' is inscribed on a stone monument at the shrine.<ref>This poem is both a nostalgic ode to his homeland and a lament before his death.</ref> Near the shrine is a pond known as "Yamato Takeru's Foot-Washing Pond," along with the "White Heron of Heguri Pond" legend and many other folk stories.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.kuwana.lg.jp/brand/bunka/rekishibunkazai/24-11220-234-410.html |title=Legends of Kuwana |access-date=2024-4-14}}</ref> The mountain behind the shrine is an ancient {{ill|Kannabi|en|Kannabi|qid=Q11588874|lt=Kannabi|1=Kannabi}}, a sacred site of worship.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.megalithmury.com/2021/05/shichi-heguri.html |title=Heguri-yama/Shichi Archaeological Site (Kuwana, Mie) |access-date=2024-4-15}}</ref>
Next to the shrine is the "Heguri Pond Park," featuring a bronze statue of Yamato Takeru.
Tsukunonokami is believed to be the same deity enshrined at {{ill|Heguri-nimasu Kishi Shrine|lt=Heguri-nimasu Kishi Shrine|lt=Heguri no Ki-uji Shrine|ja|平群坐紀氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri no Ki-uji Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1919019/1/149 |title=Tokusen Shinmyōchō |access-date=2024-4-14}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1919019/1/65 |title=Tokusen Shinmyōchō |access-date=2024-4-15}}</ref> The architecture of the shrine in Kuwana is said to be modeled after that of the Heguri Shrine in Nara. The latter originally enshrined the ancestor of the Heguri clan, but now venerates {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}.<ref>It is also suggested that the Heguri clan worshipped Ōyamatsumi.</ref>
Furthermore, various regions such as {{ill|Hei District, Chiba|en|Hei District, Chiba|qid=Q5688731|lt=Heigun District (former)|1=Hei District, Chiba}}, formerly located in {{ill|Awa Province (Chiba)|en|Awa Province (Chiba)|qid=Q1053044|lt=Awa Province (Chiba)|1=Awa Province (Chiba)}}, and Heguri settlements in {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|qid=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province (Fukuoka)|1=Chikuzen Province}}, {{ill|Hyūga Province|en|Hyūga Province|qid=Q513737|lt=Hyūga Province (Miyazaki)|1=Hyūga Province}}, and {{ill|Iyo Province|en|Iyo Province|qid=Q907829|lt=Iyo Province (Ehime)|1=Iyo Province}}, are believed to have connections to the Heguri clan.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E5%B9%B3%E7%BE%A4%E9%83%B7-3081173 |title=Heguri Settlements |access-date=2024-4-18}}</ref>
== Shrine Grounds == * The main hall (honden) is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} architectural style. * There is a worship hall (haiden) and shrine office (shamusho).
== Annual Festivals == * The main festival (例祭) is held over two days, on October 11 and 12.
== Access == * From JR or Kintetsu Kuwana Station, take a bus towards {{ill|Fukuōzan|ja|福王山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukuōzan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Happu Bus) for 20 minutes and get off at Heguri Shrine-mae.
== References ==
{{translated page|ja|平群神社 (桑名市)|version=100299740|comment=Imported full ja history}}
<references group="lower-alpha"/>

== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]
'''Heguri Shrine (Kuwana) (Q133871968)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Inabe District|en|Inabe District|ja|員弁郡|ceb|Inabe-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی ئینابێ|es|Distrito de Inabe|fa|شهرستان اینابه، میه|fr|District d&#x27;Inabe|id|Distrik Inabe, Mie|it|Distretto di Inabe|ko|이나베군|pl|Powiat Inabe|uk|Повіт Інабе|zh|員辨郡|lt=Inabe district|qid=Q1131499|1=Inabe District}} || {{ill|Heguri Shrine (Kuwana)|lt=Heguri Shrine|ja|平群神社 (桑名市)|qid=Q133871968|1=Heguri Shrine (Kuwana)}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|35.051541|136.613926|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181319 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q133871968}}==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> [[da:d:Q97220447]] [[ja:平群神社 (桑名市)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kuwana, Mie]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Heguri clan]] [[Category:History of Kuwana, Mie]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
